methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kvertus, a Defense & Security company at Growth stage founded by Yaroslav Filimonov and Andrii Znaichenko, has raised approximately $130M and is currently fundraising. The target operates within a sector containing 21 peer companies, though the comp universe for narrow valuation matching is sparse: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers identified, including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints at the same stage and similar valuation tier. Filtering the Defense & Security universe by growth-stage and capital-raise magnitude yielded fewer than three comparable transactions, making reliance on peer multiples unreliable. The existing comp set—while directionally useful for sector context—does not provide sufficient precision for quantitative triangulation.

Given these constraints, valuation of Kvertus cannot be credibly supported by traditional peer benchmarking. A rigorous analysis would require either (i) expansion of the comp set through secondary data on private fundraising rounds within the defense-tech vertical, (ii) application of discounted cash flow or revenue-multiple methods using company-specific unit economics, or (iii) explicit documentation of valuation range assumptions tied to market timing and investor risk appetite rather than public market anchors. Without additional quantitative inputs, no supported valuation range can be stated.
